output_handling.overview: askBob returns Bob's analysis as a String. The stage's job is to make that string visible in the Jenkins console and preserved as a build artifact. This guide covers the standard patterns for capturing, displaying, and archiving that output.

core_pattern.description: The single canonical pattern: capture the return, echo it between banners, write it to a markdown file, and archive that file as an artifact.

rule.title: Always capture askBob's return value
rule.description: askBob returns a String. If you ignore the return, the analysis is lost — nothing else displays it.
rule.bad: // Analysis is computed and thrown away askBob(prompt, 'mode-slug')

rule.title: Don't pipe-redirect askBob's output
rule.description: askBob is a Groovy method, not a shell command. You can't redirect its output with > or 2>. Use the return value.
rule.good: def analysis = askBob(prompt, 'mode-slug') writeFile file: 'bob-output.md', text: analysis

rule.title: Wrap Bob output in banner lines
rule.description: Jenkins build logs are noisy. Banner lines around Bob's output let a developer find it instantly when scanning the log.

rule.title: Keep the banner content stable
rule.description: Use the same banner format across stages (a row of ═ or ─, the stage name, another row). Consistency makes scanning the log faster than novelty.

pattern.name: single_artifact
pattern.description: Archive one file per Bob stage
pattern.example: writeFile file: 'bob-pr-review.md', text: analysis archiveArtifacts artifacts: 'bob-pr-review.md', allowEmptyArchive: true

pattern.name: multiple_artifacts
pattern.description: If a stage produces multiple Bob outputs, archive them with a glob
pattern.example: writeFile file: 'bob-pr-review.md', text: review writeFile file: 'bob-pr-security.md', text: security archiveArtifacts artifacts: 'bob-*.md', allowEmptyArchive: true

best_practice.title: Use allowEmptyArchive: true
best_practice.description: With allowEmptyArchive, a missing file doesn't fail the stage. Pairs with catchError-style resilience (see 5_error_resilience.xml).

best_practice.title: Pick filenames that match the stage
best_practice.description: bob-pr-review.md, bob-test-analysis.md, bob-security-report.md, etc. The filename is what shows up in the Build Artifacts list — make it self-describing.

output_processing.description: Sometimes you want to react to what Bob said — for example, mark the build UNSTABLE if the output mentions a high-risk change. Keep this simple; Bob's free text isn't a structured contract.
output_processing.caveat: If you find yourself writing a lot of regex against Bob's output, that's a signal: have the mode produce structured (JSON / YAML) output and parse that instead. Don't ad-hoc regex over markdown.

issue.name: output_not_in_console
issue.symptom: Stage runs successfully but no Bob output in the build log.
issue.cause: The return value wasn't captured or wasn't echoed.

issue.name: artifact_missing
issue.symptom: Build Artifacts list doesn't include the Bob output file.

causes.cause: writeFile path and archiveArtifacts pattern don't match
Stage failed before reaching archiveArtifacts (move it to a post block)

issue.fix: Verify the same filename in writeFile and archiveArtifacts; consider moving archiveArtifacts to post { always { ... } } so it runs even when the main steps fail.

issue.name: extra_chatter_in_output
issue.symptom: Output includes Bob's tool-trace or thinking output, not just the analysis.
issue.cause: askBob calls bob with --hide-intermediary-output. If you've modified the helper or are calling bob directly, the flag may be missing.
issue.fix: Restore the askBob helper as defined in the Jenkinsfile; don't bypass it.
